findings.message: New dependency `requests` is unpinned (no version specifier). All other runtime dependencies in this manifest carry a lower bound (e.g. `tenacity>=9.0`, `pybreaker>=1.2`). An unpinned entry lets resolvers pull arbitrary/incompatible versions and weakens supply-chain reproducibility.
findings.suggestion: Add a lower bound consistent with the rest of the manifest, e.g. `requests>=2.32`.

findings.message: `requests` is a synchronous HTTP client being added to an async FastAPI service. Calling it from request handlers will block the event loop. `httpx` is already present (in the dev group) and is the idiomatic async client for this stack — it can fully replace `requests` for both sync and async call sites, avoiding a new top-level dependency that duplicates capability.
findings.suggestion: Drop `requests` and promote `httpx` from the dev group to `[project].dependencies` (e.g. `httpx>=0.28`); use `httpx.AsyncClient` from handlers and `httpx.Client` where sync is needed.

findings.message: `requests` is in long-term maintenance mode (security/bugfix only, no new features) per its maintainers. Adopting it for a new service is a supply-chain/health regression vs. an actively developed client like `httpx`, especially given the rest of the stack is modern-async (FastAPI, OpenTelemetry, SQLAlchemy 2.x).
findings.suggestion: Prefer `httpx` (already a transitive/dev dependency) for new HTTP client work; reserve `requests` for legacy integrations that genuinely require it.
